evidence.correctness_and_freshness: Uses Flutterwave v3 API endpoints (api.flutterwave.com/v3/transactions/{id}/verify) and flutterwave-react-v3 hooks (useFlutterwave, closePaymentModal), which are internally consistent. rave_python is imported in requirements but not actually used in code — minor inconsistency. Versions claimed (rave_python==1.4.2, flutterwave-react-v3 v1.3.3). Long-tail handling: consistent + version claimed.
evidence.executability: Fenced blocks parse cleanly. Backend uses httpx directly (not rave_python despite requirements). Imports complete. CORS configured. Test cards documented. No sandbox-infeasible patterns (no ngrok/sudo). Webhook endpoint included but signature verification stubbed. Minor: rave_python listed but unused — won't break execution.
evidence.completeness: All 6 pieces present: (a) API key acquisition with dashboard path, (b) pip+npm install in bash, (c) .env templates for backend and frontend with exact names matching code references, (d) backend FastAPI code, (e) frontend React code, (f) test command with curl/Postman examples and test cards. Env vars match between listed and referenced.
evidence.citation_anchoring: Sources include flutterwaveinc.mintlify.app (not the canonical developer.flutterwave.com), GitHub repos, npm registry, and a third-party blog. No citations to the canonical developer.flutterwave.com domain. Citations only at end, not anchored near code.

rubric_notes: rave_python listed in requirements but backend implements verify via raw httpx — minor inconsistency with constraint 'rave-python current' but doesn't break execution. Citations use mintlify mirror rather than canonical developer.flutterwave.com.
